description: BTC intra-month volatility: first-half vs second-half of calendar month
hypothesis: Bitcoin daily volatility is uniformly distributed across first-half (days 1-15) and second-half (days 16-31) of the calendar month.

description: S&P 500 and BTC volume surge days (top 10%) co-occur more than chance
hypothesis: S&P 500 top-decile-volume days and BTC top-decile-volume days are independent events
detail: btc_thresh=5352704 sp_thresh=4583970 a(both)=19 b(btc_only)=44 c(sp_only)=32 d(neither)=407 n=502

description: Ethereum trade count distributions differ significantly when Bitcoin volatility is high vs low
hypothesis: ETH trade count has identical distribution regardless of Bitcoin volatility level

description: Ethereum TVL inflow events concentrate in specific lunar phases
hypothesis: Ethereum TVL growth days (TVL[t] > TVL[t-1]) are uniformly distributed across lunar phases
detail: new=36 waxing=154 full=39 waning=157 unmatched=0 growth_days=386

description: Bitcoin extreme price volatility concentrates in specific calendar months
hypothesis: Bitcoin extreme volatility days (top 5% |close-open|/open) are uniformly distributed across calendar months
detail: n=731 extreme=36 p95=0.0531 Jan=4 Feb=2 Mar=9 Apr=1 May=3 Jun=3 Jul=1 Aug=7 Sep=0 Oct=1 Nov=3 Dec=2

description: Ethereum trades at higher premium to Bitcoin when Bitcoin volume spikes
hypothesis: Ethereum-to-Bitcoin price ratio is independent of Bitcoin trading volume
detail: n=731 vol_median=1669750 ratio_median_1e6=54745 high_vol_high_ratio=154 high_vol_low_ratio=212 low_vol_high_ratio=212 low_vol_low_ratio=153

description: Bitcoin volume distribution shifts when S&P 500 volume is elevated
hypothesis: Bitcoin volume distribution is independent of S&P 500 volume level

description: BTC closing price distribution: full moon vs other lunar phases
hypothesis: BTC closing price frequency distributions are identical during full-moon and non-full-moon days
